div.head: Memorandum by the Chief of the Division of American Republics (Duggan)
div.p: The Ambassador interpreted this telegram to mean that his Government is not unfavorable to the Uruguayan proposal; that it does not, however, have any intention of recognizing Franco, and that since on the basis of the information his Government has regarding the probable reply of the United States it appears that the United States will not accept the Uruguayan proposal, Brazil will wait until all the countries have replied and then will endeavor to harmonize its reply with the others, particularly with the reply of the United States.
